claim: Multi-agent orchestration is the bottleneck, not individual agent capability; running 10-100 agents in parallel requires new human management skills (visibility, reactivity, autonomy delegation).
actionable: For multi-agent systems, prioritize orchestration UI and coordination mechanisms; individual agent strength matters less than system visibility and human oversight.
source_title: AgentCraft: Putting the Orc in Orchestration — Ido Salomon

claim: Visualizing agent work via file system mapping and heat maps reveals collisions and allows proactive prevention; agents working on the same files can be coordinated or serialized.
actionable: Build observability into multi-agent systems: map file changes to agents, visualize concurrent edits, alert on collisions before they corrupt work.
source_title: AgentCraft: Putting the Orc in Orchestration — Ido Salomon

claim: Campaign orchestration (decompose task once, agents execute autonomously in container) reduces human babysitting; humans move from planning to review phase.
actionable: For high-volume agent work, use campaign/batch patterns: plan once, execute many agents in parallel, review results; reduces human context switching.
source_title: AgentCraft: Putting the Orc in Orchestration — Ido Salomon

claim: Review bundles with visual evidence (screenshots, videos) allow humans to evaluate agent work without rewatching everything; human review becomes scalable.
actionable: For agent review workflows, bundle changes with visual artifacts (screenshots, diffs); use filtering and batch review to scale human oversight.
source_title: AgentCraft: Putting the Orc in Orchestration — Ido Salomon

claim: Human-agent collaboration via shared workspaces enables team parallelization; humans and agents can hand off work and see each other's progress in real-time.
actionable: For team + agent workflows, implement shared workspaces where both can read/write same files and task context; enables smooth handoff and discovery.
source_title: AgentCraft: Putting the Orc in Orchestration — Ido Salomon
